source: Outputs/readout_html/necromancer_readout.html's --accent CSS variable, cross-confirmed against Outputs/class_sheets/ability_sheets_all_classes.html's embedded per-class JSON. Part of an already-established per-class accent system covering all 21 classes (spot-checked: Reaper #0a876b, Pyromancer #ff6112, Chronomancer #ffed4a all differ) - not invented for this folder.
accent_rgba_note: 0-1 float conversion of accent_hex (45/db/9c / 255), ready to plug directly into a WeakAuras color field (e.g. a Class Accent icon's 'color', or an aurabar's 'barColor') - no manual hex-to-float conversion needed at fill time.
usage: Class Accent Left/Right icons (Template_shadow.py's Row C.5, currently generic placeholders)
Any Necromancer-specific aurabar's barColor, if a class-tinted resource bar is ever wanted over the default red/white scheme
subglow's glowColor, if a class-tinted proc glow is ever wanted over the default white

fantasy_mechanic.summary: Commanding a growing roster of raised/summoned undead minions - the Necromancer directs (Command: X), the minions fight. Class-level, not per-spec: confirmed 2026-07-06 by Battlewrath checking the CoA talent builder directly - even Rime (nominally the frost-control spec) still leans on pets heavily, so this isn't specific to the Animation tree despite Animation's own talent names (Animate: X, Raise: X) making the pet-focus most explicit there.
fantasy_mechanic.source: Battlewrath, 2026-07-06, direct observation from the Conquest of Azeroth talent builder tool - not independently re-derived from Spell.dbc data.
fantasy_mechanic.status: Provisional - first pass at this field. Not yet used to override the insight/decision tracking rule (slot_assignment.md) - currently documentation/context only. Revisit if a pet-roster-specific HUD element (e.g. minion-count or guardian-stack tracking, see spell_index.md's shelved 'Guardian count buff' finding) ever gets built - that would be the natural place this field starts actively mattering.
fantasy_mechanic.note_for_other_classes: This field is new as of 2026-07-06 and only exists for Necromancer so far - not yet backfilled for any of the other 20 classes' theme.json-equivalent (most don't have one yet at all).
